sections.label: Hook
sections.text: KHK is usually introduced as the liver enzyme that processes fructose. But this paper shows it has a second job.
sections.speaker_directions: Start measured and curious. Slight emphasis on “second job.” Pause briefly after the final phrase.

enhancement_cues.description: Title card: “KHK has a second job” with KHK highlighted in blue.

sections.label: Nutrient stress setup
sections.text: When hepatocytes are overloaded with fructose and saturated fat, the endoplasmic reticulum—the cell’s protein-folding workspace—comes under pressure.
sections.speaker_directions: Professional, explanatory pace. Define “endoplasmic reticulum” clearly without rushing.

enhancement_cues.description: Fructose and palmitate icons flow into a hepatocyte; the ER workspace fills with folding protein shapes.

sections.label: Adaptive pathway
sections.text: In that stress state, KHK rises alongside the adaptive IRE1 alpha–XBP1 branch of the unfolded protein response.
sections.speaker_directions: Slow slightly on pathway names. Treat “adaptive” as the key word.

enhancement_cues.description: Pathway diagram: Nutrient overload → KHK ↑ → IRE1α-XBP1 adaptive response → ER chaperone support.

sections.label: Cross-species evidence
sections.text: The same axis appeared in HepG2 cells, primary hepatocytes from GAN diet-fed mice, and human MASLD liver biopsies.
sections.speaker_directions: Use a confident list rhythm: cells, mice, humans. Emphasize “same axis.”

enhancement_cues.description: Three evidence cards animate in: HepG2 cells | GAN-diet mice | MASLD patient biopsies.

sections.label: The twist
sections.text: Then came the twist: knocking down KHK reduced this balance, but increased misfolded proteins, ubiquitination, and PERK-CHOP apoptotic signaling.
sections.speaker_directions: Shift to a more dramatic, lower tone on “the twist.” Keep technical list crisp and separated.

enhancement_cues.description: Split screen: left adaptive XBP1 path dims; right misfolded protein aggregates and red PERK-CHOP apoptosis path intensify.

sections.label: Therapeutic implication
sections.text: So KHK is not just a metabolic gatekeeper. It may also be a proteostasis safeguard that helps liver cells survive nutrient stress.
sections.speaker_directions: Clear aha moment. Emphasize the contrast between “gatekeeper” and “safeguard.”

enhancement_cues.description: Two role labels appear beside KHK: “fructose flux gatekeeper” and “ER proteostasis safeguard.”

sections.label: Landing and CTA
sections.text: That means future KHK therapies may need precision: reduce harmful fructose flux without losing ER protection. Read the open-access paper at the DOI shown here.
sections.speaker_directions: Concise closing. Slow down on “precision.” Finish with a confident call to action.

enhancement_cues.description: Final card: paper title, authors, journal, DOI 10.1152/ajpgi.00235.2025.

metadata.approval_source: User said: Approve Concept 1 with Remotion

findings.title: Duration is slightly above nominal 60 seconds
findings.description: The script targets 63 seconds to preserve technical clarity at a moderate professional pace.
findings.action: If strict 60.0 seconds is required, trim the final CTA or compress the cross-species evidence line.

summary.success_criteria_met: Script covers approved key points, includes 7 enhancement cues, and stays within approximately 10% of the 60-second target.
